snippet.title: Pastors' Point of View (PPOV) 231. Faith & Public Policy. Andy Woods & Rick Miller. 10-28-22.
snippet.description:
On the eve of a historic midterm election in the USA, Dr. Andy Woods and guest, the Hon. Rick Miller (former member of the Texas House), discuss the importance of Christians being involved in voting and public policy. Here, we discuss why such a responsibility flows from believers needing to be good stewards of our free republic and also functioning as biblical salt and light. Also, we discuss today’s unfortunate fragmented evangelical mind that separates the secular from the sacred. We also talk about the personal sacrifice that was needed to get our country off the ground and how such self-sacrifice will also be needed to turn our country around.

https://mailchi.mp/andywoodsministries/ppov-231-notes

www.andywoodsministries.org

www.spiritandtruth.org

www.slbc.org

www.chafer.edu

snippet.title: Hampshire Cricket's partnership with the Charity
snippet.description:
Becoming a corporate partner of Southampton Hospitals Charity is your opportunity to not only make a significant difference to patient care at University Hospital Southampton, but it can also boost your company, as seen through our partnership with Hampshire Cricket. 

Taking part in fundraising activities and events can boost staff morale and support the development of your employees, giving them new experiences and skills. What better way to get your team motivated and working together? 

Supporting a Charity can help enhance awareness of your brand and demonstrate your commitment to Corporate Social Responsibility, while directly benefiting one of the leading hospitals in the country. 

To find out more, email charity@uhs.nhs.uk.

snippet.title: Brian Flores' Effect During Steelers OTAs on Myles Jack/Devin Bush / Chase Claypool Growing Up?
snippet.description:
The Pittsburgh Steelers OTAs wrapped up their first week, and we got our first look at linebackers coach Brian Flores working closely with Devin Bush and Myles Jack. Jack revealed Flores joining the Steelers helped him pick Pittsburgh in free agency, and it's clear there's going to be some up-close work for the Steelers linebackers with Flores.

Also, Chase Claypool spoke about having more responsibility in the Steelers locker room as a third-year veteran. As the only Steelers receiver from 2021 at the 2022 OTAs, he's taking on more of a leadership role and helping George Pickens adjust to the NFL.

Meanwhile, Pickens sounds confident, but not cocky, as he starts his NFL career while the young offensive players are just starting to get to know each other, with funny stories and interactions between players like Pat Freiermuth and Kenny Pickett.

Brian Flores' Effect During Steelers OTAs on Myles Jack/Devin Bush / Chase Claypool Growing Up? https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC7JorgD8nGLtvvMy1ogFetg

snippet.title: Chris Turner - The Geography of Hope
snippet.description:
Chris Turner, author of The Geography of Hope: A Tour of the World We Need, a survey of innovations and initiatives around the world that are moving us towards a sustainable future.

Chris Turner is a journalist, best-selling author, and a sought after public speaker. His determination to find tangible solutions to climate change has injected new hope into the environmental movement.

Chris was born in 1973 in Moose Jaw Saskatchewan, into a military family. He has lived in the Canadian North, the American Midwest, Germany, and India. Chris studied history at Queen’s University in Kingston Ontario, and journalism at Ryerson, but his obsession with culture and technology led him to Shift Magazine, one of the more influential Canadian magazines of the 1990s. He started there as an intern but stayed on after his 1998 graduation from Ryerson until 2003.

--
This interview is part of The Green Interview, an archive of resources pertinent to an understanding of the future of life on earth and humanity’s roles and responsibilities in sustaining it. The archive was produced by the late Silver Donald Cameron and Chris Beckett during the 10 years before Cameron’s death in 2020. Through the efforts of family and friends and the generosity of private donors, The Green Interview has been made freely available to all who would find it of benefit. Its permanent home is with the Science, Environment and Economy Archives of Library and Archives Canada.

Books by Silver Donald Cameron: http://www.silverdonaldcameron.ca/writer/books/

#Economics #Journalism #ClimateChange #TheGreenInterview
snippet.channelTitle: The Green Interview - Re-invent the world

snippet.title: Child Serial Killer, David Penton: Case Study Pt. 1
snippet.description:
Are there more victims?
#ChristiMeeks #AngelicaGandara #ChristieProctor #AraJohnson #ShannonSherrill #RoxannReyes #NydraRoss #DavidPenton #SerialKiller

VICTIM 1: In November, 1984, Michael James Penton, 8 weeks old, was violently shaken to death by his father, David Penton. He was convicted of manslaughter in his son's death.
Reference: 
Newspapers.com

VICTIM 2: On January 19, 1985, Christi Lynn Meeks, age 5, was abducted while playing with other children outside her Mesquite, Texas apartment. Her body was found on April 3, 1985 in Texoma Lake, near the Oklahoma/Texas border. David Penton was convicted of her murder and given a life sentence.
References: 
Newspapers.com
Gray Hughes Investigates
Logan Wade - Alex Murdaugh - Christi Lynn Meeks - Hatchet Attack 1977

VICTIM 3: On Sunday, July 14, 1985, Angelica Maria Gandara, age 11, was abducted while walking in the vicinity of 2nd and 4th Street in Temple, Texas. Angelica is described as having long brown hair, brown eyes, 5'1'' tall and weighing 110 pounds. She was last seen wearing a black and white shirt with the brand name Sassoon written across the chest, black shorts, white socks, and white tennis shoes. Angelica has not been found. David Penton is listed as a suspect in her missing person’s case on the Charley Project website but has not been charged.
References:
Newpapers.com
Charley Project
https://charleyproject.org/case/angelica-maria-gandara
NamUs
https://www.namus.gov/MissingPersons/Case#/6096

VICTIM 4:  On Saturday, February 15, 1986 Christie Dianne Proctor, age 9, was abducted while walking to her Woodside Terrace apartment on Brookgreen Dr, in Dallas, Texas. Her remains were found on April 9, 1988 in a field on Plano Parkway, Plano, Texas, just 8 miles from her home. David Penton was convicted of her murder and given a life sentence.
Reference: 
Newspapers.com

VICTIM 5: On April 2, 1986, Ara Denise Johnson, aged 5, was abducted from her mobile home, 300 block of Boulder Street in Big Sandy, Texas, in the middle of the night. Her father checked on her around 2:30 am. Ara was gone, along with her dark orange blanket by 6:30am. She was dressed only in her underwear and her hair was in pigtails. She was described as being 3 feet tall and 39 pounds. She has black/dark brown hair and brown eyes. She was missing two of her lower front teeth and had a filling in her right back molar. Ara went by the nickname “Neecee.” She has not been found. David Penton alleges that he abducted and killed Ara but has not been charged.
References:
Newpapers.com
Film Daily News
https://filmdaily.co/news/ara-johnson/ 
NamUs
https://www.namus.gov/MissingPersons/Case#/6227/contacts?nav

VICTIM 6:  On Sunday October 5, 1986, Shannon Marie Sherrill, age 6, abducted from her home, W Plum Street, Thorntown, Indiana. Shannon was playing hide and go seek with neighborhood friends near her front yard. She is described as a white female, 3 feet tall, 30 pounds, brown hair and blue eyes. She has an extremely shy nature. She was responsible and would not go with a stranger or leave the yard, according to her parents. Shannon has not been found. David Penton implied that he was going to be charged but has yet to be convicted or named as a suspect.
TIPS- Thorntown Police Department at (765) 436-7677 or Retired Detective Jeff Heck at (765) 516-4087
References:
Newpapers.com
NCMC
https://www.missingkids.org/poster/NCMC/601901/1/screen
Charley Project
https://charleyproject.org/case/shannon-marie-sherrill
NamUs
https://www.namus.gov/MissingPersons/Case#/6651/details?nav 
Facebook- Buried Cold Cases- Shannon Sherrill
https://www.facebook.com/groups/buriedcoldcases/permalink/2554089804683234/
Facebook- Never Forget Me- Shannon Sherrill
https://www.facebook.com/WeWontForgetThem 
 
VICTIM 7: On November 3, 1987, Roxann Hope Reyes, age 3, was abducted from an alley behind her parents Meadow Terrace apartment in Garland, Texas. Her remains were found on May 23, 1988 in a field in Murphy, Texas. David Penton was convicted and given a life sentence.
References:
Newspapers.com

VICTIM 8: On March 31, 1988, Nydra Antoinette Ross, age 9, was abducted from her aunt's front yard, on 17th Ave, in Columbus, Ohio. Her remains were found on September 24, 1988 in a creek near Gearhiser and Prospect-Mt-Vernon roads, Ohio. David Penton was convicted and sentenced to life. He is eligible for parole in 2027. If given parole, he will be transferred to Texas to begin serving his 3 life sentences for the other murders he plead guilty to, listed above.
References:
Newspapers.com


Cold Truth Links

Link Tree-This link will take you to all the places you can connect with me!
https://linktr.ee/ColdTruth
Buy Me A Coffee/Banana
https://www.buymeacoffee.com/coldtruthpw
